criteria.criterion: A single `delete_events`-style helper exists in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` that acquires `write_lock`, checks the rebase guard, and routes git through the shared index-lock retry.
The helper's `git commit` is pathspec-scoped to the deleted paths (cannot sweep unrelated staged residue), and it cleans up on failure.

All four sidecar `prune()`s (`plan_review/sidecar.py`, `code_review/sidecar.py`, `completion_sidecar.py`, `overlap/digest_sidecar.py`) call the helper instead of raw `subprocess.run(["git", ... "rm"/"commit"])`.
Each caller retains its best-effort exception-swallowing posture (a failed prune never fails the gate).
A unit/interface test covers the helper: it deletes only the named events, commits under the lock, and does not sweep an unrelated staged file in the index.
